title: cassia yucatanensis
intro: Cassia yucatanensis, also known as Mexican or Yucatan cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to Mexico and Central America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant has anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Cassia yucatanensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal benefits, and its leaves and bark are also used in herbal preparations for topical applications.
